sources.title: Advancing the National Adaptation Plan of the Maldives (NAP Maldives)
Government Budget PSIP Summary

P-MEE061-100.summary: No public source confirms this PSIP line item. Given its tiny budget (~USD 30,000) and single-month window (Dec 2020), it is likely a minor/generic Ministry of Environment budget line rather than a named program. Recommend RTI request to Ministry of Finance / Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy.

P-MEE001-112.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment (Project Management Unit), with WAMCO as operational partner

sources.title: 51077-002: Greater Male Environmental Improvement and Waste Management Project
51077-003 (Waste-to-Energy)
Ministry of Environment project page
Expected completion late 2026 - Maldives Financial Review
GMEIWMP Audit Report 2024

P-MEE001-112.summary: ADB/AIIB/IsDB-financed Greater Male Environmental Improvement and Waste Management Project (GMEIWMP), covering Thilafushi dumpsite remediation and Male'/Villimale'/Hulhumale' transfer stations; ongoing, completion expected late 2026.

P-MEE001-114.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment and Energy, with WAMCO operating facilities

sources.title: World Bank Project Detail - P160739
World Bank press release 2017
Vandhoo RWMC handover to WAMCO
Consultations Plan - MCEP Zone V and IV

P-MEE001-114.summary: World Bank-financed (P160739, US$17.5M IDA grant) nationwide Maldives Clean Environment Project covering Regional Waste Management Centers (e.g. Vandhoo in Raa) and Island Waste & Resource Management Centers across Zones II/IV/V (Dhaalu, Faafu, Laamu, Meemu, Thaa) - not a single atoll/island. Still active per June 2025 procurement documents; World Bank flagged implementation challenges at Vandhoo facility.

P-MEE062-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy (POISED PMU), with STELCO/FENAKA

sources.title: ADB 46122-005: POISED Additional Financing
POISED 2024 Financial Statement Audit
POISED - Ministry of Tourism and Environment

P-MEE062-001.summary: Likely a late-stage/closeout-year (2025) financial activity of the multi-island POISED renewable energy program. POISED spans 8+ atolls and up to 160 outer islands, so no single atoll/island applies. Verify exact nature via RTI.

P-MEE062-100.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment (POISED PMU), with STELCO and FENAKA Corporation

sources.title: Project Spotlight: POISED - CIF
ADB 46122-003: POISED
POISED - Ministry of Tourism and Environment

P-MEE062-100.summary: ADB/CIF-financed POISED renewable energy program installing solar-battery-diesel hybrid systems across ~70-160 outer islands in 8+ atolls; explicitly multi-island national scope, no single-island attribution appropriate.

P-WST088-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology / GMEIWMP Project Management Unit

sources.title: 51077-002: Greater Male Environmental Improvement and Waste Management Project
Greater Male' Environmental Improvement and Waste Management Project
Expected completion late 2026

P-WST088-001.summary: Continuation entry of the ADB/AIIB/IsDB-financed GMEIWMP program, same as P-MEE001-112, covering Greater Male region and outer islands; ongoing, completion expected late 2026.

P-WST089-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology - Waste Management Department

sources.title: Maldives Clean Environment Project - World Bank Documents
Consultations Plan - MCEP Zone V and IV

P-WST089-001.summary: Same nationwide World Bank-financed Maldives Clean Environment Project as P-MEE001-114, spanning multiple waste management zones rather than a single island.

P-WST110-001.lead_institution: Waste Management Corporation Limited (WAMCO), with Kulhudhuffushi City Council and MACCS

sources.title: New Facility Aims to Make Kulhudhuffushi First Plastic-Free Urban Centre
Maldives opens new material recovery facility in Kulhudhuffushi

P-WST110-001.summary: Waste management infrastructure/material recovery facility upgrade in Kulhudhuffushi City, Haa Dhaalu atoll, developed under the PLEASE project via WAMCO/City Council/MACCS partnership.

sources.title: WAMCO - Waste Management Facilities

P-WST272-129.summary: WAMCO operates waste collection/billing in Fuvahmulah City (Gnaviyani atoll); no specific source confirms this exact FY2025 infrastructure upgrade line item. Verify via RTI.

P-WST035-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology, with WAMCO, financed via World Bank Maldives Clean Environment Project

sources.title: Consultations Plan - MCEP Zone V and IV

P-WST035-001.summary: Ministry tender for bio-treatment of organic waste pilot systems in 4 islands of Zone 4 & 5 under MCEP; exact islands not confirmed publicly. Verify via RTI.

P-OTH007-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology, with Addu City Council

sources.title: Regional Air Quality Monitoring Project launches - Times of Addu

P-OTH007-001.summary: Regional Air Quality Monitoring Project launched Feb 2022, first sensor installed in S.Hithadhoo, Addu City; 5-year data collection program, part of a 10-site nationwide network.

P-MEE001-105.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology

sources.title: The ASPIRE Project - World Bank
ASPIRE Program - CIF

P-MEE001-105.summary: ASPIRE is a World Bank/CIF-financed national program (2014) mobilizing private solar investment across multiple islands (6.5MW installed); not tied to a single island, winding down/completed by 2024-2025, succeeded by ARISE.

P-WST032-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy, with EPA, URA, WAMCO; financed by ADB/AIIB/Japan Fund for Poverty Reduction

sources.title: 51077-003: Greater Male Waste-to-Energy Project
Govt launches Greater Male Waste to Energy Project worth USD 304 million

P-WST032-001.summary: Greater Male Waste-to-Energy Project (500 tpd plant) at Thilafushi, Kaafu Atoll; under construction as of 2025, completion expected late 2026.

P-WST032-002.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy, with EPA, URA, WAMCO

sources.title: 51077-003: Greater Male Waste-to-Energy Project

P-WST032-002.summary: Same Thilafushi Greater Male Waste-to-Energy Project as P-WST032-001, a later-year (2025) continuation of construction spending.

P-WST011-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy / WAMCO, OFID-financed Zone 1 program

sources.title: Environment Ministry virtual session with MATI on waste management

P-WST011-001.summary: 'Zone 1' refers to Haa Alif, Haa Dhaalu and Shaviyani atolls (41 inhabited islands), OFID-financed. Spans three atolls, no single atoll/island can be assigned; verify via RTI.

P-MEE001-111.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment / Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy

sources.title: Maldives begins transforming waste to energy at Vandhoo
Maldives produces electricity from waste in historic first
ADFD-funded waste-to-energy project in Maldives

P-MEE001-111.summary: Likely the small-scale waste-to-energy facility at Vandhoo, Raa Atoll (ADFD/IRENA financed, ~$6M, operational 2019), serving Noonu/Raa/Baa/Lhaviyani atolls; a related 'Phase 2' (Abu Dhabi-funded, multiple islands) may also exist per unverified budget portal search snippets. Verify via RTI.

sources.title: Provision of Water Supply, Sanitation and Solid Waste Management Project - OPEC Fund
Zone 1 waste management definition
Loan Agreement signed with OFID

P-MEE002-018.summary: Zone 1 (Haa Alif, Haa Dhaalu, Shaviyani atolls) component of the OFID-financed water/sanitation/waste program (US$50M loan, status Completed per OPEC Fund), covering construction/equipping of 21 island waste management centres. Spans multiple islands, no single location; verify via RTI.

sources.title: Zone 1 waste equipment context

P-MEE002-019.summary: Sibling line item to P-MEE002-018, procuring waste management equipment for Zone 1 (HA/HDh/Sh) island waste centres; short 6-month 2019 window suggests a completed equipment delivery order.

sources.title: On tap: How the Maldives is restoring water security on its most vulnerable outer islands

P-MEE015-006.summary: No direct source ties this code to a specific island. Timing/title consistent with MEE-led community water storage tank installations on outer islands 2019-2020. Recommend RTI to Ministry of Finance/Environment.

P-MEE054-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment and Energy (likely with National Disaster Management Authority)

sources.title: A Water Crisis in Paradise - The Weather Channel
More than 20 islands already facing water shortages

P-MEE054-001.summary: Likely the government's recurring seasonal emergency water-provision program (desalinated/bottled water shipments to islands during dry season), historically covering dozens of islands nationwide - the 'single_island' scope tag is likely mislabeled. Recommend RTI.

P-DRG004-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology

sources.title: Maldives Budget - S. Maradhoofeydhoo
Maradhoo-Feydhoo - Wikipedia

P-DRG004-001.summary: Maradhoofeydhoo is a district/island of Addu City, Seenu (S) Atoll. Budget portal lists a drainage project here at feasibility/design stage as of the 2022 budget cycle; a much larger Addu City-wide drainage buildout was later completed (~2024-2025), possibly superseding this standalone project. Verify current status via RTI.

P-ENV006-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy, with EPA as operational/regulatory implementer

sources.title: South Ari Marine Protected Area (SAMPA)
Maldives - Green Climate Fund
South Ari Atoll MPA - Wikipedia

P-ENV006-001.summary: SAMPA (South Ari Marine Protected Area) spans a 42km reef corridor in Alif Dhaalu (ADh) atoll from Rangali to Dhigurah, the Maldives' largest MPA and a major whale shark aggregation site. 2025 activity includes a new protection decree, vessel registration, and ranger training - operationalizing an amended management plan, not initial establishment. No single island applies since it's a multi-island reef corridor.

P-HTE122-002.lead_institution: Ministry of National Planning, Housing and Infrastructure

sources.title: Gov't seeks public opinion on Male' drainage systems project
Won't increase drainage capacity - Sun Online

P-HTE122-002.summary: No source directly names this project. Contemporaneous 2019-era Male' drainage expansion work targeted Henveiru and Maafannu wards under the Ministry of National Planning, Housing and Infrastructure. Presumed completed given the Dec 2019 end date; exact ward/phase unconfirmed. Verify via RTI.

P-ACT004-003.lead_institution: Ministry of Fisheries, Marine Resources and Agriculture (inferred)

sources.title: Green Fund used to address coconut shortage - Atoll Times
Gov't begins planting 5,000 coconut palm trees - PSM News

P-ACT004-003.summary: Likely an early phase of the Green Fund-financed National Coconut Palm Rehabilitation Programme, led by the Ministry of Fisheries, Marine Resources and Agriculture, distributing palms nationwide. A closely-matching 2023 program (different dates) confirms the pattern but not this exact 2021-2022 tranche. Multi-location by nature; verify via RTI.

P-ACT001-022.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy

sources.title: Maldives Protected Areas portal
Addu Atoll Biosphere Reserve Action and Conservation Plan

P-ACT001-022.summary: No project formally named 'Southern Marine Park Management Project' was found. Most plausibly relates to marine protected area management in Addu Atoll (Seenu) and/or Fuvahmulah biosphere reserves. Verify via RTI to Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy.

P-OTH006-001.lead_institution: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), under Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy

sources.title: Terms of Reference for Air Quality Monitoring Toolkit, Ministry of Environment

P-OTH006-001.summary: Project title specifies Male', consistent with Kaafu Atoll/Male City. EPA is the agency responsible for ambient air quality monitoring; exact 2024 station referenced could not be independently confirmed - verify with EPA or via RTI.

P-CPT048-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy, with JICA, financed by Green Climate Fund

sources.title: FP165: Building Climate Resilient Safer Islands in the Maldives - GCF
Building Climate Resilient Safer Islands in the Maldives (BCRSI)

P-CPT048-001.summary: Building Climate Resilient Safer Islands (BCRSI) is a real, ongoing GCF/JICA-financed program (FP165) running through ~2030, focused on coastal protection. Public documentation identifies target sites in Laamu Atoll and Addu City - not Baa/Raa as the recorded data hints - so this record's specific island(s) could not be confirmed. Verify via RTI.

sources.title: 51077-003: Greater Male Waste-to-Energy Project

P-WST033-001.summary: No company, program, or project named 'Arena' related to waste-to-energy in the Maldives could be found. Likely a small island-council-level pilot or vendor-named contract not indexed publicly. Recommend RTI request.

P-WST036-001.lead_institution: Waste Management Corporation Limited (WAMCO), under Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy

sources.title: WAMCO - Waste Management Corporation Limited

P-WST036-001.summary: WAMCO's 'Zone 2' waste catchment spans Noonu, Raa, Baa, and Lhaviyani atolls (hub facility at Vandhoo, Raa), not Seenu as the recorded hint suggests. No source ties this exact project to a single island within that 4-atoll zone. Verify via RTI/WAMCO.

P-WST038-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy / WAMCO

sources.title: About Our Waste Management Project - Ministry of Environment

P-WST038-001.summary: 'Zone 3' waste catchment spans Greater Male, Kaafu, Alifu Alifu, Alifu Dhaalu, and Vaavu atolls (Thilafushi facility). No source ties this exact project to a single island. Verify via RTI/WAMCO.

P-WST011-002.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology

sources.title: Zone 1 definition - Times of Addu
Maldives Budget 2023 - HA. Kelaa

P-WST011-002.summary: Zone 1 island waste management centre upgrades, spanning Haa Alif, Haa Dhaalu and Shaviyani atolls. No single island confirmed for this exact budget line. Verify via RTI.

P-WST045-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology (WAMCO role unconfirmed)

sources.title: Kolamaafushi - Wikipedia
President meets GA. Kolamaafushi Island Council

P-WST045-001.summary: Atoll/island directly confirmed by project title: GA (Gaafu Alifu), Kolamaafushi. As of a Feb 2024 presidential visit, the council still cited waste management difficulties, suggesting the facility was not yet operational then despite the project's mid-2023 budget window.

P-WAS013-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology (operator - Fenaka vs MWSC - unconfirmed)

sources.title: Isles.gov.mv Government Island Registry: An'golhitheemu
Maldives Budget 2021 - R. Angolhitheemu
President Muizzu Vows Action on Angolhitheemu's Development Concerns

P-WAS013-001.summary: Angolhitheemu is confirmed to be in Raa (R) Atoll per the government island registry and budget portal. A 2024 news report on a presidential visit indicates the water/sanitation network remained an unresolved community concern well after this project's stated 2023-04 end date, suggesting it ran past schedule or is incomplete. Verify via RTI.

P-MEE001-110.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment and Energy, executed with UNDP

sources.title: From scarcity to sustainability: Maldives' ambitious water security project - GCF
FP007: Supporting vulnerable communities in Maldives to manage climate change-induced water shortages
Water Supply Project at HDh. Nolhivaranfaru Inaugurated
Handover of Water Networks in Raa Maduvvari and Baa Dharavandhoo - UNDP

P-MEE001-110.summary: Very likely the Maldives Green Fund's counterpart-financing line for the GCF/UNDP 'Supporting Vulnerable Communities in Maldives to Manage Climate Change-Induced Water Shortages' program (FP007): 4 water hub islands (HDh.Nolhivaranfaru, Sh.Foakaidhoo, R.Maduvvari, B.Dharavandhoo) plus 25 rainwater harvesting islands across 13 atolls, serving ~106,000 people. Dates (Jun 2019-Jun 2022) match the hub islands' inauguration/handover timeline closely. Genuinely multi-atoll; no single location applies.

P-MEE083-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment and Energy, with WAMCO as eventual operator

sources.title: WAMCO to manage Addu City waste
ICB tender notice: Hithadhoo Regional Waste Management Facility design/construction

P-MEE083-001.summary: Appears to cover two parallel city-level waste components - Addu City (sited at Hithadhoo) and a separate Fuvahmulah facility - as part of a 2017-2019 build-out led by the Ministry of Environment with WAMCO as eventual operator. Genuinely spans two cities (Seenu and Gnaviyani); no single atoll/island applies. Verify via RTI.

P-MEE084-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment (Waste Management & Pollution Control Department), operations via WAMCO

sources.title: President tours Waste Management Centre in Gdh. Thinadhoo
Regional Waste Management Strategy and Action Plan for Zone 6 Maldives - UNEP/IETC

P-MEE084-001.summary: Huvadhu Atoll's regional waste program is a real multi-year effort under the Ministry of Environment/WAMCO, centered on a regional transfer facility at GDh.Vaadhoo alongside an island-level center at GDh.Thinadhoo. A 2023 sector policy document still described the region relying on manual waste handling, suggesting the system is delayed/ongoing. No source ties this code to one specific host island. Verify via RTI.

sources.title: Agreement signed to install water supply and sewerage systems in five islands
Water supply, sewerage projects to commence in five islands in Baa Atoll

P-SAN011-001.summary: No source directly matches this project's code, budget, or 2019-2023 window. Comparable 'sewerage in 5 islands' projects found for this era are multi-atoll rather than confined to Seenu (Addu), so the recorded 'S' atoll hint may be a coding artifact. Recommend RTI request to identify the actual island list.

P-SAN028-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Construction, Housing and Infrastructure, with Ministry of Finance and Planning

sources.title: OFID: Outer Islands Harbors, Water Supply and Sewerage Facilities Project
Auditor General - Outer Islands Harbors Project (Phase III) 2020-2023 Financial Statement Audit

P-SAN028-001.summary: Most likely OFID's 'Outer Islands Harbors, Water Supply and Sewerage Facilities Project' (approved 2019, labeled Phase III), spanning 13+ islands across five atolls (Baa, Shaviyani, Haa Dhaalu, Gaafu Dhaalu, Gaafu Alifu), executed by the Ministry of Construction, Housing and Infrastructure. Genuinely multi-atoll; no single location applies.

P-WAS031-001.lead_institution: Ministry of National Planning, Housing and Infrastructure, implemented by MWSC

sources.title: State contracts MWSC with projects worth over MVR 1 billion
Kunburudhoo (Haa Dhaalu Atoll) - Wikipedia

P-WAS031-001.summary: Matches ADh.Kunburudhoo, contracted to MWSC (MVR 45.12M) as part of a ~MVR 1 billion national multi-island water/sewerage program. Note a separate Kunburudhoo also exists in Haa Dhaalu Atoll - verify exact atoll via RTI.

P-WAS045-001.lead_institution: Ministry of National Planning and Infrastructure, implemented by Static Company / MWSC

sources.title: Agreement signed to install water supply and sewerage systems in five islands
Kanduhulhudhoo Council

P-WAS045-001.summary: A March 2019 agreement for water/sewerage in GA.Kanduhulhudhoo and Th.Kinbidhoo (~MVR 38M total) closely matches this project's budget and start date. Note this may be the same island already mapped elsewhere in the dataset as GA.Kanduhulhudhoo - flag as likely duplicate and verify via RTI.

P-WAS050-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Planning, Housing and Infrastructure, implemented by Male' Water and Sewerage Company (MWSC)

sources.title: Water and sewerage project at Hirimaradhoo officially inaugurated
Projects - MWSC
MWSC awarded water and sanitation projects in 24 islands

P-WAS050-001.summary: MWSC's official projects page lists HDh.Hirimaradhoo as 'Completed [2024]', matching the project's 2019-12 to 2024-12 window. Contracted for MVR 69.14M total (Green Fund's MVR 41.9M likely a portion of that).

sources.title: Majlis approves US$11,122,700 loan for Fuvahmulah water and sewerage
VP inaugurates powerhouse and sewerage restoration in Fuvahmulah City

P-WAS061-001.summary: Fuvahmulah's (Gnaviyani atoll) water and sewerage network, built by Sierra Construction (partly Kuwait Fund-financed), operational by 2017-2019, consistent with this line item's 2019 window.

sources.title: Addu City's sewerage finally close to completion after 10 years
Operations of Addu sewerage system handed over to Fenaka

P-WAS062-001.summary: No source directly matches this project code/budget. Addu City's much larger sewerage network (OFID/MTCC-linked, MWSC-built, later handed to Fenaka) is likely a separate project; this may be a discrete design/feasibility component. Verify via RTI.

P-WAS067-001.summary: No independent public record found for this project. Small budget and short timeline suggest a minor PSIP line item (feasibility study or materials procurement). Verify via RTI.

P-WAS092-001.lead_institution: Ministry of National Planning, Housing and Infrastructure

sources.title: Outer Islands Harbors, Water Supply and Sewerage Facilities Project - OPEC Fund
Loan Agreement signing - Phase III - Ministry of Finance
Contracts Signed to Develop Water Supply and Sewerage System in 34 Islands

P-WAS092-001.summary: Confirmed as OFID Phase III 'Outer Islands Harbors, Water Supply and Sewerage Facilities Project', a US$50M loan signed Jan 2020 covering ~13 outer islands across multiple atolls (including Haa Alif, Baa, Alif Dhaal, Thaa). Genuinely multi-island by design, no single atoll/island applies.

P-WST007-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy, with WAMCO as operator

sources.title: Bidding notice: Regional Waste Management Facility, Hithadhoo/Addu City
WAMCO to manage Addu City waste

P-WST007-001.summary: Corresponds to the Regional Waste Management Facility (waste-to-energy plant, ~$28M) at S.Hithadhoo, Addu City. Construction underway since 2018; no confirmation of full completion by Dec 2024. Verify via RTI.

P-WST007-002.lead_institution: Ministry of Climate Change, Environment and Energy

sources.title: Thilafushi Waste Management Centre will address regional waste issues: Minister

P-WST007-002.summary: Tiny budget relative to P-WST007-001 suggests a minor closeout/consultancy line item related to one of the regional waste facility zones (Addu, Thilafushi, or Vandhoo), but no direct evidence ties it to a single site. Verify via RTI.

sources.title: ADFD and IRENA to finance a waste to energy project in Maldives

P-WST033-002.summary: No project named 'Irena Converting Waste to Energy' found; likely a data-entry artifact referencing IRENA (International Renewable Energy Agency, co-financer of the Vandhoo facility) rather than a distinct project name. Verify via RTI.

P-WST087-001.lead_institution: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Technology

sources.title: Maldives begins transforming waste to energy at Vandhoo

P-WST087-001.summary: Tiny budget (~$300) and one-month window suggest a residual/closeout line item within the long-running national 'Small Scale Waste to Energy Project' program (same title as P-MEE001-111), not a new island facility. Verify via RTI.
